---
title: "Kagi API and openalexVectorise corpus handoff"
author: "Rainer Krug"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Kagi API and openalexVectorise corpus handoff}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  eval: false
---

# The Kagi API surface

The current `kagiPro` surface targets the Kagi API described by the bundled
OpenAPI spec at
`system.file("api_specs/openapi.yaml", package = "kagiPro")`. Two endpoints:

- `POST /search` — search with workflows (search, images, videos, news,
  podcasts) and rich filters / lens / extract options.
- `POST /extract` — turn 1–10 URLs into markdown.

Authentication uses `Authorization: Bearer <key>` against
`https://kagi.com/api/v1`.

```r
library(kagiPro)

conn <- kagi_connection(
  api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi")
)
```

## Search

The `/search` endpoint accepts a typed JSON body; `kagi_query_search()`
builds it:

```r
q <- kagi_query_search(
  "biodiversity loss",
  workflow = "search",
  limit    = 50,
  filters  = list(region = "DE")
)

kagi_fetch(
  connection     = conn,
  query          = q,
  project_folder = "kagi_demo"
)
```

This writes:

```
kagi_demo/
└── search/
    ├── json/                       # raw API responses (audit trail)
    │   └── query_1/
    │       ├── _query_meta.json
    │       └── search_1.json
    └── parquet/                    # analysis-ready Hive-partitioned parquet
        ├── query=query_1/type=search/...
        ├── query=query_1/type=image/...
        └── ...
```

The `/search` response carries many typed arrays (`search`, `image`,
`video`, `news`, `infobox`, `direct_answer`, `related_search`, ...); each
non-empty type lands in its own `type=...` partition.

## Extract

`/extract` turns one or more URLs into markdown. The API takes 1–10 URLs per
request; `kagi_query_extract()` auto-chunks longer vectors:

```r
q_ext <- kagi_query_extract(c(
  "https://example.com/article-1",
  "https://example.com/article-2"
))

kagi_fetch(conn, q_ext, project_folder = "kagi_demo")
```

The parquet output has one row per extracted URL with the `url` and `markdown`
columns.

## Corpus handoff to openalexVectorise

The `openalexVectorise` package expects a corpus with `id`, `title`, `abstract`
columns at `<project_dir>/corpus/`. The `as_corpus_parquet()` adapter maps a
search parquet dataset onto that shape (using the `snippet` field as
`abstract` by default):

```r
parquet_path <- kagi_fetch(conn, q, project_folder = "kagi_demo")

as_corpus_parquet(
  input       = parquet_path,
  project_dir = "/tmp/oavec_demo"
)
# → /tmp/oavec_demo/corpus/ is ready for openalexVectorise::embed_corpus()
```

For a one-step workflow, pass `corpus = list(project_dir = ...)` directly to
`kagi_fetch()`:

```r
kagi_fetch(
  connection     = conn,
  query          = q,
  project_folder = "kagi_demo",
  corpus         = list(project_dir = "/tmp/oavec_demo")
)
```

Then:

```r
openalexVectorise::embed_corpus(
  project_dir = "/tmp/oavec_demo",
  backend     = <chosen-backend>
)
```
